rulings.Case Title: LADVA, SHOKER & ASSOCIATES VS. NAVEEN SINGHA
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Second) Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 17, 2010, line 1, PLAINTIFF LADVA, SHOKER & ASSOCIATES (Second) Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order. GRANTED, NO OPPOSITION FILED. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ALBERT G SWANSON VS. FREDERICK BOST DR et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 17, 2010, line 1, DEFENDANTS FREDERICK BOST DR., CALIFORNIA PACIFIC ORTHOPAEDICS & SPORTS MEDICINE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTS THE BURDEN OF PROOF WITH DR. SAMPSON'S DECLARATION. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. CAUSATION MUST BE PROVEN WITHIN A REASONABLE MEDICAL PROBABILITY BASED UPON COMPETENT EXPERT TESTIMONY. MERE POSSIBILITY ALONE IS INSUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH A PRIMA FACIE CASE. SEE JONES V. ORTHO PHARMACEUTICAL CORP. (1985) 163 CAL.APP.3D 396. PLAINTIFF'S EXPERT DECLARATIONS FROM DOCTORS SLATER AND SMITH DO NOT MEET THIS STANDARD. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY VS. CHUBB CUSTOM INSURANACE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 17, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANT LEXINGTON INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. DENIED. LEXINGTON'S MOTION ARGUES QUESTIONS OF FACT. ALSO, THERE IS A CLEAR DISPUTE OVER THE CORRECT APPLICATION AND INTERPRETATION OF THE POLICY. CHUBB'S OBJECTION TO REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS SUSTAINED. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINE PETERSON VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-10-500217 WITH CASE CGC-09-494798
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 17, 2010, line 4, PLAINTIFF CHRISTINE PETERSON'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-10-500217 WITH CASE CGC-09-494798. GRANTED. PLAINTIFF SUSTAINED SIMILAR INJURIES, FRACTURES TO HER RIGHT WRIST, IN TWO SLIP AND FALL ACCIDENTS THREE MONTHS APART. TREATMENT FOR HER INJURIES WAS COMPLICATED BY THE NEW FRACTURE BEING SUPERIMPOSED ON THE PRIOR FRACTURE, COMPLICATING THE APPORTIONMENT OF HER INJURIES BETWEEN THE TWO ACTIONS. ALL PARTIES WOULD BE PREJUDICED UNLESS THE CASES ARE CONSOLIDATED. CASES ARE CONSOLIDATED FOR ALL PURPOSES WITH CGC-09-494798 AS THE LEAD CASE. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINE PETERSON VS. PHILIP DIAZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Christine Peterson'S Motion To Transfer
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 17, 2010, line 13, DEFENDANT CITY OF BERKELEY'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE TO ALAMEDA COUNTY SUPERIOR COURT. DENIED. ALTHOUGH CCP SECTION 394 CONFERS UPON A PUBLIC ENTITY THE RIGHT TO CHANGE OF VENUE TO THE COUNTY WHERE THE ACCIDENT OCCURRED, THESE CASES MUST BE CONSOLIDATED TO AVOID PREJUDICE TO ALL PARTIES. THE CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISO HAS A COMPETING AND EQUAL RIGHT UNDER CCP SECTION 394 TO HAVE ITS CASE HEARD IN SAN FRANCISCO. THE ENDS OF JUSTICE DICTATE THAT THE CASES BE HEARD TOGETHER. (CCP SECTION 397.) =(302/CWW)
